info.description: Sovereign European BaaS — lightweight open-source Supabase alternative. Provides JSON document database (PebbleDB), JWT/OAuth authentication, file storage (local + S3), realtime WebSocket, queues, and config maps — all in a single ~40 MB binary.
info.title: Sovrabase API

get.description: List documents in a collection with optional filtering, field selection, and pagination. Query parameters are treated as filter fields.

parameters.description: Comma-separated list of fields to return
parameters.name: select

parameters.description: Maximum number of documents to return (paginated response)
parameters.name: limit

parameters.description: Number of documents to skip (paginated response)
parameters.name: offset

200.description: List of documents or paginated response

post.description: Insert a new document into the specified collection. An _id is auto-generated if not provided.
Execute multiple insert, update, and delete operations in a single atomic batch.
Query documents in a collection with a structured filter, field projection, and pagination.
Perform a full-text search on documents in a collection.

get.description: Retrieve a single document by its ID from the specified collection.

put.description: Partially update an existing document by ID. Supports both PUT and PATCH methods.

delete.description: Delete a document by ID from the specified collection.

get.description: Returns all config entries for the project.

post.description: Creates a new config entry (POST) or updates an existing one (PUT). On successful create returns 201, on update returns 200.

get.description: Returns all public config entries as a simple key→value map. No authentication required.
Returns a single config entry by its key.

put.description: Creates a new config entry (POST) or updates an existing one (PUT). On successful create returns 201, on update returns 200.

delete.description: Deletes a config entry by its key.

post.description: Accepts a batch of analytics events for processing. Returns 202 with count of accepted events.

get.description: Get the profile of the currently authenticated user.

post.description: Deletes a message by its ID from the specified queue (acknowledges receipt).
Receives up to `limit` messages from the specified queue. Messages are made invisible until deleted or timeout.
Sends a JSON message to the specified queue. Returns the message ID and queue name.

get.description: List files in a storage bucket, optionally filtered by prefix.

post.description: Upload a file to the specified storage bucket. Supports multipart form data with optional path.

get.description: Download a file from the specified storage bucket. Supports optional image transformation parameters.

delete.description: Delete a file from the specified storage bucket.

post.description: Send a password reset email to the specified address. Returns a reset token.
Generate a magic link for passwordless authentication. Returns a token for the magic link.
Confirm MFA setup by providing a valid TOTP code. Returns backup codes. Requires authentication.
Disable MFA for the authenticated user. Requires a valid TOTP code. Requires authentication.
Generate an MFA secret and URI for TOTP setup. Requires authentication.

get.description: Check whether MFA is enabled for the authenticated user.
Get the OAuth authorization URL for the specified provider. Optionally redirects the browser if redirect=true.

parameters.description: Set to 'true' for HTTP redirect instead of JSON response
parameters.name: redirect

get.description: Called by the OAuth provider after user authentication. Reads code and state from query params, exchanges for tokens, and redirects the browser with tokens in the URL fragment.

post.description: Exchange a refresh token for a new access and refresh token pair.
Reset a user's password using a reset token and new password.
Authenticate a user with email and password. Returns access and refresh tokens.
Create a new user account with email and password. Returns the created user and authentication tokens.
Verify a user's email address using a verification token.
Verify a magic link token and return authentication tokens.

ProjectKey.description: Project API key for multi-tenant isolation. Required for all /auth/v1 and /api/v1 endpoints.
ProjectKey.name: X-Project-Key
